---
title: "SPM Monorepo Architecture"
---

## Directory Structure

```
macos/
├── Package.swift                          # Root manifest
├── Packages/
│   ├── NanoCore/                          # Logging, config
│   ├── NanoDesignKit/                     # Brand colors
│   ├── NanoImageKit/                      # Gemini API + image processing
│   └── NanoStorageKit/                    # SwiftData persistence
├── Sources/
│   ├── NanoMeme/                          # Library target (all app logic)
│   │   ├── App/                           # AppDelegate, AppState, Coordinator
│   │   └── UI/                            # MenuBar, Settings views
│   └── NanoMemeApp/                       # Executable (@main)
├── Tests/NanoMemeTests/                   # App-layer tests
├── Resources/                             # Info.plist, entitlements
└── justfile                               # Build pipeline
```

## Package.swift

The root manifest uses Swift tools version 6.0 with language mode v5 (for compatibility with libraries that aren't fully Swift 6 ready):

```swift
// swift-tools-version: 6.0
import PackageDescription

let package = Package(
    name: "NanoMeme",
    platforms: [.macOS(.v14)],
    products: [
        .library(name: "NanoMemeLib", targets: ["NanoMeme"]),
        .executable(name: "NanoMeme", targets: ["NanoMemeApp"]),
    ],
    dependencies: [
        .package(path: "Packages/NanoCore"),
        .package(path: "Packages/NanoDesignKit"),
        .package(path: "Packages/NanoImageKit"),
        .package(path: "Packages/NanoStorageKit"),
        .package(url: "https://github.com/sindresorhus/KeyboardShortcuts", from: "2.0.0"),
    ],
    targets: [
        .target(
            name: "NanoMeme",
            dependencies: ["NanoCore", "NanoDesignKit", "NanoImageKit",
                          "NanoStorageKit", "KeyboardShortcuts"],
            swiftSettings: [.swiftLanguageMode(.v5)]
        ),
        .executableTarget(name: "NanoMemeApp", dependencies: ["NanoMeme"]),
        .testTarget(name: "NanoMemeTests", dependencies: ["NanoMeme"]),
    ]
)
```

::: {.callout-note}
## Why `.swiftLanguageMode(.v5)`?
Swift 6's strict concurrency checking conflicts with KeyboardShortcuts' static lazy initialization pattern. Using v5 language mode gives us the SPM 6.0 features while keeping concurrency warnings non-fatal.
:::

## Dependency Graph

Each package has a single responsibility:

| Package | Responsibility | Dependencies |
|---------|---------------|--------------|
| **NanoCore** | Logging, config, UserDefaults wrapper | None (Foundation only) |
| **NanoDesignKit** | Brand colors, design tokens | None |
| **NanoImageKit** | Gemini API, text overlay, Keychain | NanoCore |
| **NanoStorageKit** | SwiftData models, export, settings | NanoCore |

## Testing Strategy

All packages have co-located test targets using Swift Testing framework (`@Suite`, `@Test`, `#expect`):

```swift
@Suite("GeminiConfig")
struct GeminiConfigTests {
    @Test("default resolution is 1K")
    func defaultResolution() {
        let config = GeminiConfig()
        #expect(config.resolution == .one)
    }
}
```

The test suite uses `MockURLProtocol` for HTTP testing and `.serialized` trait to prevent race conditions between suites sharing mock state.
